segments.text: Moreover the LORD answered Job, and said,
Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then Job answered the LORD, and said,
Behold, I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Behold, I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea, twice; but I will proceed no further.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then answered the LORD unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,
Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Wilt thou also disannul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Deck thyself now with majesty and excellency; and array thyself with glory and beauty.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one that is proud, and abase him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Look on every one that is proud, and bring him low; and tread down the wicked in their place.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hide them in the dust together; and bind their faces in secret.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Behold now behemoth, which I made with thee; he eateth grass as an ox.

footnotes: behemoth: probably an extinct animal of some kind
heading: Description of Behemoth

segments.text: Lo now, his strength is in his loins, and his force is in the navel of his belly.
He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.
His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.
He is the chief of the ways of God: he that made him can make his sword to approach unto him.
Surely the mountains bring him forth food, where all the beasts of the field play.
He lieth under the shady trees, in the covert of the reed, and fens.
The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.
Behold, he drinketh up a river, and hasteth not: he trusteth that he can draw up Jordan into his mouth.
He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.

footnotes: Will any take him in his sight, or, bore his nose with a gin?
